text:
It was a starless November night when Jack decided to hit the town. He had saved enough money to afford a modest night on the town after months of saving pennies and practicing his waitressing skills. He swung by an old jazz club in the part of town he barely recognized.

The music pulsed from inside as patrons danced to the cool, lazy groove of a big band. Jack took a seat at the bar, nursing a cheap beer while watching a group of older patrons who seemed comfortably relaxed despite the cold. A middle-aged man in a tattered suit with a weather-worn mustache leaned back in his chair and cracked a joke that made the entire bar laugh.

Unusual for him, Jack laughed too.

"Hey," the man called over, wiping tears from his eyes. "You gotta live a little, don't ya?"

Jack hesitated. The world felt colder without the festive glow. But something about the elderly wit made him nod agreeably.

"I suppose," Jack replied
